rationale: Relative consistency is built from the consistency predicate Con on the two theories; def:axiom-systems-consistent-axiom-system is the only consistency node available and carries the definition. 'Let T0, T1 be theories' merely types the objects, and the 'equivalently ... contradiction' clause restates consistency (contradiction is already internal to it), so neither theory nor contradiction is a separate load-bearing edge.
notes: Mild type mismatch: the consistency node is phrased for an 'axiom system' while the statement applies Con to 'theories'. No 'consistent theory' node exists (closed world), so this is the correct target; flag for the reviewer in case a theory-level consistency node is later authored.
